title: Verdant Vale
_comment: A demo score for the Stage 1 engines — the deliberate opposite of chiptune. Karplus-Strong plucks (guitar/harp) give real plucked-string tone, FM gives a Rhodes-like electric piano and inharmonic bells, and the strings/lead use vibrato + chorus + convolution reverb for an analog, breathing feel. Nothing here is a bare square wave.

guitar._comment: Nylon-ish plucked string via Karplus-Strong. Real attack and natural decay, no oscillator can fake this.

harp._comment: Brighter, faster-decaying pluck for rolling arpeggios.

ep._comment: FM electric piano (Rhodes-like): 1:1 ratio, modulator fades for a struck, bell-tinged attack.

bell._comment: FM bell with an inharmonic 3.5 ratio — shimmering, metallic, decays to nothing.

strings._comment: Supersaw string pad warmed by vibrato (delayed onset, like a section swelling) and widened by chorus into convolution reverb.

lead._comment: Expressive solo voice: resonant analog filter + vibrato that eases in mid-note.

bass._comment: Round triangle sub, lightly filtered — felt more than heard.

vale_theme._comment: 8 beats / 2 bars in D major. The pastoral hook — stated in full in the title, fragmented elsewhere.

breeze._comment: 4 beats. A gentle descending answer phrase that carries continuity without restating the hook.
